mod_name: A Bumpy Ride - 日本語翻訳

changelog.1.0.0: Initial release of the Japanese translation of A Bumpy Ride. ~900 strings translated (UI, tutorial, achievements, shop, quests, skin descriptions, title screen catch phrases, tutorial dialogues, freight tooltips, settings menu, end-of-day screen, popups). Register: ですます調 (standard polite form, casual gaming convention). Conventions: proper names in EN (train skins, stations, regions), shop signs in EN (western atmosphere), imperial units preserved (FT, miles), On/Off not translated. Functional parity with FR/DE/ES v1.0.0 baseline. SpecialPassenger.uexp remains vanilla (62 Shareholder task strings stay in EN) for crash-safety reasons documented in TRANSLATION_RULES.md section 2.5.ter.
changelog.1.0.1: Font fix for CJK glyph rendering. The vanilla game uses bitmap UFont atlases (Pixel_Times_Font, AwfullyDigital_Font, Cavalhatriz_Font, Pixel_Times_Bold_Font) that only contain Latin glyphs - all Japanese characters rendered as empty tofu. Fix: 4 font assets overridden with a composite Font cloned from Engine/EngineFonts/Roboto.uasset, which chains RobotoRegular (Latin) + DroidSansFallback (CJK fallback already embedded in Unreal Engine). All Hiragana/Katakana/Kanji now render correctly via the engine's built-in CJK fallback. Trade-off: the original pixel-art / digital aesthetic of Pixel Times and AwfullyDigital is lost - all UI text now uses Roboto. Game-internal bitmap fonts (Pixel_Times_Bold_Font_Signs for station signs and Universal_Serif_Font) are left vanilla since station names and shop signs are kept in EN anyway.
changelog.1.0.2: Crash fix and missing strings. (1) Fix Actionnaire (Shareholder) pickup crash: SpecialPassenger.uasset/.uexp reverted to vanilla in the build, as patching it triggers an infinite script recursion in UE5 (same root cause documented in FR/DE v1.4.4 - the 62 Shareholder task strings stay in EN). (2) Add 10 strings that were present in the binary but missing from the original JSON extract (added manually in FR/DE v1.3.3+): QuestBoard 'Click to lock' -> 'クリックでロック', 'Click to unlock' -> 'クリックでロック解除', TrainScreen 'Watch out for tornadoes!' -> '竜巻に注意!', 'You are lost!' -> '迷子になりました!', 'Tracks may be slippery!' -> '線路が滑りやすいかも!', 'Water usage is twice as fast!' -> '水の消費が2倍速くなります!', 'Click to drop explosives' -> 'クリックで爆薬を投下', NewShopMenu 'OWNED' -> '所持済', 'PAINT' -> '塗装', EndOfDayScreen_Paper 'Money made today: ' -> '本日の売上: '.
changelog.1.0.3: Real fix for Actionnaire pickup crash. v1.0.2 reverted SpecialPassenger but the crash persisted: the actual culprit was QuestTicket.uasset, patched in JP but kept vanilla in ES v1.4.6 for the exact same reason (EX_Jump offset corruption on change-of-length destination strings - JP destinations are longer than EN, breaking the bytecode jump targets). DE manages to patch it because of how its strings happen to align byte-wise, but JP cannot. Fix: QuestTicket.uasset/.uexp now reverted to vanilla in the build, matching ES policy. Cost: the destination panel on quest tickets (DESTINATION:, Destination: Nearest Station) stays in EN. Comparison audit added to staging/_package_jp.py post-fixes to prevent regression. SpecialPassenger also kept vanilla (62 Shareholder task strings stay in EN), same as v1.0.2.
changelog.1.0.4: Translation parity with FR/DE v1.4.5 via BPOffsetPatcher. Instead of reverting SpecialPassenger and QuestTicket to vanilla (v1.0.2/v1.0.3 stopgap), both are now properly translated using the BPOffsetPatcher tool that was created in FR v1.4.5 specifically to solve the EX_Jump/EX_SwitchValue offset corruption that KissE's placeholder+branch approach introduces. BPOffsetPatcher rewrites strings edit-in-place, builds a global shift map, and updates all absolute jump-like opcodes (EX_Jump, EX_JumpIfNot, EX_PushExecutionFlow, EX_SwitchValue.EndGotoOffset + Cases.NextOffset) plus all internal callers' EX_IntConst entry-points. Result: 61 SpecialPassenger strings translated (Actionnaire tasks: See the sunset -> 夕日を見る, etc. - 2 'AM'/'PM' skipped due to known duplicate bug from FR v1.4.5) + 3 QuestTicket DESTINATION strings translated (DESTINATION: -> 目的地:). QuestTicket is a first-time use of BPOffsetPatcher on a non-SP Blueprint - same fix profile (EX_Jump offset corruption by change-of-length), 7 callers shifted. Both confirmed compiling without crashing the asset load.
changelog.1.0.5: Fix Actionnaire QuestBoard duplicates: second occurrence of ' law signs', ' hours', and ' times' was left in EN in v1.0.4 because the BPOffsetPatcher wrapper dedup'd the input JSON by Original string. DE/FR v1.4.5 had not dedup'd (they passed all 72 SP entries through, with duplicates), letting BPOffsetPatcher patch all bytecode occurrences. Wrapper now keeps duplicates -> 64 SP strings patched (vs 61 in v1.0.4). AM/PM stay in EN intentionally (translation identical to source, universal abbreviation kept across all languages).
changelog.1.4.7: Multi-language alignment with FR/DE/ES v1.4.7. No functional change vs JP v1.0.5 (same binaries: 61 SpecialPassenger strings + 3 QuestTicket strings translated via BPOffsetPatcher, font overrides for CJK rendering, 0 'law signs' in EN). JP joins the unified release tag v1.4.7 with FR/DE/ES.
